title: silene willdenowii subsp. prostrata
intro: Silene willdenowii subsp. prostrata is a prostrate-growing subspecies of the Silene willdenowii plant, a species of flowering plant in the carnation family. It is also known as creeping silene or prostrate campion, and is native to certain regions of the world. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The herbal preparations of Silene willdenowii subsp. prostrata, typically in the form of infusions or decoctions, have been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory infections, and to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
